step2_w2f_mapping.semantics: W2.F triages each candidate URL into H/M/L tiers via Haiku. H → INSERT INTO sources (auto-approved). M → INSERT INTO provisional_sources (queued-provisional). L → audit log only. Re-running on stale provisional rows requires either (a) calling verifyCandidate directly with the existing URL/name from the stale row, then if H is the outcome, promoting/marking the stale row reviewed, or (b) calling /api/admin/sources/promote with admin auth. Direct verifyCandidate path keeps the script self-contained and uses the same Haiku classifier the production endpoint uses.
step2_w2f_mapping.re_run_strategy: For each stale row: (i) call verifyCandidate with skipDuplicateCheck=true (existing provisional row would otherwise re-trip duplicate logic), (ii) inspect the returned tier, (iii) update the stale row in-place — set status='confirmed' if H, refresh recommended_tier from Haiku output, mark reviewed_at NOW(). The stale row is what gets refreshed; W2.F doesn't normally touch existing provisional rows, so we adapt its logic for re-classification while staying within the helper module.

step4_battery_brief.brief_excerpt_first_2k:
# EU Battery Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2023/1542)
## Regulatory Fact Document

---

# Purpose and Scope of This Document

This document analyses Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 12 July 2023 concerning batteries and waste batteries ("the Battery Regulation"), published in the Official Journal of the European Union L 191, 28 July 2023, currently in force in its consolidated version of 31 July 2025.

The brief is anchored to a workspace operating as a **freight forwarder** across the Americas, Europe, and Asia trade lanes, with cargo verticals spanning live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian. Air transport is the primary mode; road is secondary; ocean is tertiary.

**Convention note:** This document applies the Integrity Rule throughout. Sections are populated only where the regulation's text, as provided in the source content, furnishes grounded claims. Sections requiring legal confirmation are explicitly labelled. No delegated acts, implementing regulations, or Commission guidance documents beyond the regulation's own text have been provided in the source pool; where those instruments would be required to complete an analysis, the gap is labelled explicitly.

**Scope limitation:** The source content provided is the preamble and early recitals of the Battery Regulation. The operative articles (beyond recital cross-references) were not fully provided. Where article-level specificity is needed, the relevant article number is cited from the regulation's structure as identified in the recitals; however, any analysis dependent on operative article text that was not supplied is labelled as a **research gap requiring primary-text verification**.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, European Parliament and Council, 12 July 2023, OJ L 191/1, 28 July 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1542/oj*

---
